Reason for the angular shape is that the thick, ultra-hard stainless steel body panels cannot be stamped like the thin, feeble, paper-strength https://t.co/mc4YRbe4J4
summary: Only when you drive the Cybertruck do you realize how incredible it is: a bulletproof tank that moves like a million dollar sports car! Reason for the angular shape is that the thick, ultra-hard stainless steel body panels cannot be stamped like the thin, feeble, paper-strength
ai_summary: Elon Musk explains Tesla's Cybertruck design rationale, specifically why the vehicle features an angular shape: the ultra-hard stainless steel body panels cannot be stamped using traditional manufacturing techniques used for conventional thin metal car bodies, requiring a fundamentally different engineering approach. This illustrates a key manufacturing and product design trade-off at Tesla—selecting materials and shapes based on production constraints rather than aesthetic preferences, revealing how material science and manufacturing capabilities directly drive product form factor decisions in automotive engineering.
embed_html:
Only when you drive the Cybertruck do you realize how incredible it is: a bulletproof tank that moves like a million dollar sports car!

Reason for the angular shape is that the thick, ultra-hard stainless steel body panels cannot be stamped like the thin, feeble, paper-strength… https://t.co/mc4YRbe4J4

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) May 7, 2026

text: The GB300 is the best AI computer https://t.co/WX6iFyjdJM
summary: The GB300 is the best AI computer
ai_summary: Elon Musk asserts that the GB300 is the best AI computer, referencing xAI's flagship hardware accelerator for large-scale AI model training and inference. This statement reflects xAI's positioning of the GB300 as a competitive alternative to NVIDIA's H100/H200 GPUs for enterprise AI compute infrastructure, relevant to entrepreneurs evaluating AI hardware options and the competitive landscape in AI infrastructure.
embed_html:
The GB300 is the best AI computer https://t.co/WX6iFyjdJM

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) May 6, 2026

text: Try Grok Voice for your customer support https://t.co/pSq9YH3eO3
summary: Try Grok Voice for your customer support
ai_summary: Elon Musk announces Grok Voice as a customer support solution, demonstrating xAI's expansion beyond conversational AI into practical enterprise applications. This reflects a key business strategy of leveraging advanced AI models to solve real operational problems for companies, positioning Grok as a competitive alternative to existing customer support tools.

ai_summary: Elon Musk responds to criticism about Grok's viability by providing concrete updates on xAI's AI development progress: multiple Grok models are simultaneously training on their Colossus 2 supercomputer infrastructure, and the Grok Built harness (likely their deployment/integration framework) is advancing well. This demonstrates xAI's commitment to iterative model development and infrastructure scaling as a counter to market skepticism about the Grok product line.
embed_html:
We have many great Grok models training simultaneously in Colossus 2.

I am confident that they will bear fruit.

And work on the Grok Built harness is progressing well.

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) May 7, 2026
